name: Randolph AFB
profile: Military-friendly Randolph AFB housing snapshot anchored to San Antonio / Universal City / Schertz, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Steady demand from military personnel and retirees

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 Texas VA loan rates.

notes: Property taxes and insurance materially affect true monthly ownership cost near Randolph.
Insurance and HOA should be included in all-in housing cost comparisons.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan estimate; higher property taxes typical for Texas are offset by zero state income tax.

crime_status.metro_shreveport_bossier: Universal City and Schertz are considered very safe; low violent crime compared to San Antonio proper.

school_quality.on_base: Randolph Field ISD serves on-base residents and is highly rated.
school_quality.off_base: Schertz-Cibolo-Universal City ISD is highly rated and preferred by off-base residents.

rental_vacancy.notes: Healthy supply of new rental homes in the I-35 corridor.

rental_metrics.notes: Healthy supply of new rental homes in the I-35 corridor.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Known as 'Military City USA'; deep community ties and massive support for JBSA personnel.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Zero state income tax offsets higher property taxes for VA loan buyers.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest in the growing Cibolo and Schertz areas.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Top-tier schools and steady military demand make this a premier relocation market.

summary_points: Joint Base San Antonio - Randolph sits in the San Antonio / Universal City / Schertz housing market with base-specific affordability, commute, and neighborhood tradeoffs.
Current market trend is approximately 1.8% year-over-year, which should shape buyer leverage and pricing discipline.
Zero state income tax offsets higher property taxes for VA loan buyers.
Neighborhood quality varies across Schertz, Cibolo, Universal City and other nearby corridors — citywide averages should not be the only filter.
Top-tier schools and steady military demand make this a premier relocation market.
opportunities: Randolph military demand can support durable housing relevance for disciplined buyers and landlords.
Local inventory and pricing may offer opportunity for households that stress-test taxes, insurance, and commute together.
Schertz and nearby corridors may offer stronger family or value positioning depending on assignment length.
Assignment-length clarity can improve rent-versus-buy decisions near Randolph.
risks: Neighborhood quality, schools, commute, and monthly carrying costs vary across the San Antonio / Universal City / Schertz market.
Property taxes, insurance, HOA, and utilities can widen the gap between list price and true monthly payment.
Market averages can hide weak submarkets — property-level review matters.
Stretching beyond a safe housing ratio can reduce PCS flexibility.
buyer_notes: Randolph buyers should compare all-in monthly cost, not just principal and interest.
Verify school zones, commute timing, and BAH fit by exact address before signing.
Strongest in the growing Cibolo and Schertz areas.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who test the commute during expected report hours.
seller_notes: Sellers should price against current inventory and days on market rather than older peak assumptions.
Move-in-ready presentation and realistic pricing matter when buyers have leverage.
Military-adjacent commute convenience can be a listing strength if framed clearly.
investor_angles: Military turnover near Randolph can support rental demand, but vacancy and maintenance should be modeled conservatively.
Investors should underwrite taxes, insurance, HOA, and property condition — not just acquisition price.
Practical commute corridors often outperform generic metro averages for military renter demand.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly payment as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, commute, school quality, and resale flexibility before locking a neighborhood.
Do not choose housing based only on distance to Randolph.
A slightly lower purchase price can materially improve monthly PCS flexibility.
seller_guidance: Price against active inventory and days on market.
Condition and presentation matter when buyers can compare more options.
Military commute convenience can help positioning if described accurately.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near Randolph.
Model vacancy, repairs, insurance, and turnover conservatively.
Stable spread matters more than headline affordability alone.

base_profile.base_bluf: Top-tier schools and steady military demand make this a premier relocation market.

user_positioning.realtor_bluf: Lead with BAH realism, commute risk, school-zone confidence, and all-in payment math for Randolph clients — not generic San Antonio / Universal City / Schertz stats alone.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Randolph is influenced by PCS turnover, training/permanent-party mix, and practical commute corridors.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Identify the most realistic primary gate.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on mileage.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window you would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Randolph housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, utilities, debt, and commute. BAH is a decision filter, not a promise of affordability.
recommended_calculations: BAH with dependents vs estimated rent
BAH without dependents vs estimated rent
Total housing cost vs 30% income lane
Residual monthly income after expenses and housing
bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter for Randolph, not a promise of affordability. Push users into PCSUnited financial calculators when the housing gap looks tight.

family_readiness.family_bluf: Families PCSing to Randolph should verify school zones, childcare options, medical access, commute reality, and housing availability before choosing a neighborhood.

base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited is not an official DoD source. This Base tab is intended for public-facing PCS orientation, housing research, and relocation planning. Users should verify gate hours, access requirements, medical services, housing availability, school zones, and official procedures directly with the installation or official service provider before travel, lease signing, or home purchase.

parser_notes: This Randolph AFB file was upgraded to the Lackland-style PCSUnited schema with slug, name, state_code, profile, market_metrics, metrics, ownership_costs, costs, rental_metrics, summary_points, risks, opportunities, buyer_notes, seller_notes, investor_angles, and guidance arrays.
San Antonio / Universal City / Schertz is preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context; base-specific market data was retained.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.

This file now includes base_profile for the PCSUnited Base tab, including gate orientation, visitor control center, major services, on-base housing, recommended nearby neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, PCS watchouts, and source notes.
